notes: 2026-04-26 19:48 America/Chicago conservative no-add pass. No app code changed. Re-read the live V2 inventory, route details, triage memory, and normalized ledger, then did one fresh Minnesota-first bounded discovery sweep. Checked three fresh official clusters: Mississippi recommended trip #1 (already live as East River Flats to Hidden Falls), Mississippi recommended trip #2 (still blocked by the same missing route-level live gauge / threshold model for Coon Rapids Dam to North Mississippi Park), and the North Fork Crow recommended trip (still duplicate corridor versus the existing Riverside-to-Dayton live route plus queued Rockford-to-Riverside segment). No genuinely new evidence changed any candidate status, so the run stopped conservatively instead of padding the queue with stale or weaker reviews. Net result: no candidate statuses changed; no tests or build were run because no product files changed.

2026-04-27: gauge-first pass reviewed fresh Minnesota DNR clusters on the South Fork Crow, Watonwan, and Redwood. South Fork Crow produced one needs-manual-coordinates candidate; Watonwan produced one research-later hold; Redwood stayed cluster-only because the lower route shape runs into Redwood Lake / dam logistics without a cleaner short recommended trip on this pass.

2026-04-26 20:28 America/Chicago conservative no-add pass. No app code changed. Re-read the live V2 inventory, route details, triage memory, and normalized ledger. No queued likely_addable candidate remains, and this run did not surface a genuinely new official route page, new official gauge guidance, new threshold evidence, or new endpoint coordinates. Stopped conservatively instead of recycling stale threshold_weak / research_later / rejected holdovers as fake fresh discovery. Net result: no candidate statuses changed; no tests or build were run because no product files changed.

gauge.notes: Direct MN DNR Garden City gauge (site 254, Watonwan River nr Garden City, CSAH13) sits on the lower route corridor and has an official interpreted ladder.

thresholdSupport.notes: MN DNR ladder for site 254: scrapable below 1.5 ft, low 1.5-1.6, medium 1.6-4.4, high 4.4-6.0, very high above 6.0. Thresholds are not the blocker here.

candidates.notes: Fresh gauge-first discovery from the Watonwan cluster. The official PDF clearly names both accesses on the same lower corridor (~14.7 river miles), but the trust trail still feels like a stitched subsegment rather than a DNR-endorsed day trip.

candidates.route: Elba to Highway 74
candidates.blocker: No defensible live gauge-plus-threshold story strong enough for PaddleTodayV2. The active Beaver gauge sits downstream, but current evidence still does not tie a usable paddling floor or range cleanly back to the Elba-to-Highway-74 reach.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if MN DNR, USGS-adjacent guidance, or strong same-route mixed evidence ties the Elba-to-Highway-74 reach to a defensible live gauge model.

gauge.notes: Active official Whitewater river-level site exists near Beaver, but route-to-gauge threshold fit is still weak for this upstream reach.

thresholdSupport.notes: Route support exists, but published numeric paddling guidance does not cleanly map to this exact segment.

candidates.notes: Re-reviewed at 12:40 America/Chicago on 2026-04-26; status unchanged and still below implementation bar.

candidates.route: Village Park to Theilman Access
candidates.blocker: The live USGS gauge at Zumbro Falls is route-adjacent and the official endpoints are now clear, but the numeric threshold support is still a stitched mixed-source story from neighboring Miles Paddled reaches rather than a clean exact-route ladder for Hammond-to-Theilman itself.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry if MN DNR, USGS-adjacent guidance, or strong same-route evidence ties the Zumbro Falls gauge directly to the Hammond/Village-Park-to-Theilman corridor, or if a fresh exact-route trip report publishes a defensible minimum/range.

aliases: Zumbro River - Village Park to Theilman Access
Zumbro River - Hammond Village Park to Theilman Access

gauge.notes: USGS 05374000 at Zumbro Falls sits upstream on the same main stem and already underpins nearby shipped Zumbro routes; this is a much cleaner same-gauge fit than the earlier vague Hammond follow-on pass.

thresholdSupport.notes: Mixed evidence is directionally promising: Miles Paddled Zumbro River II says 7-9 ft is recommended, 12 ft is high, and 5 ft was still adequate for the Zumbro Falls-to-Hammond family, while Zumbro River III calls 5.2 ft the lowest recommended level for the downstream Millville-to-Theilman family. That is not clean exact-route support for the official Hammond-to-Theilman route, so it stays below likely-addable.

candidates.notes: Fresh Minnesota-first same-gauge follow-on from the Zumbro Falls cluster. The current DNR Map 2 text clearly names Village Park at Hammond and Theilman carry-in access, which fixes the endpoint-name blocker from the earlier 16:58 pass, but the threshold package is still a neighboring-reach stitch rather than a clean route-specific model.

candidates.route: County Road I to County Road G
candidates.blocker: A live USGS story likely exists somewhere on the Crawfish system, but exact bridge-access authority is still too thin and I still do not have official or strong same-route threshold evidence for this route.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if county, state, or water-trail materials clearly support both bridge accesses and a defensible live gauge model emerges for this exact reach.
